reviews.review_description: Great game and thoroughly enjoyable, BUT like a lot of others (even checked on discord) I have hit the mission bug where there is no transfer button  when you're supposed to press it. Completely stuck at this point. Shame.
immersive, wonderful, brilliant, a must have for all sci-fi enthusiasts. a fun and not too complex engineering masterpiece absolutely worth buying

reviews.review_title: This game needs a major update
reviews.review_description:
This game needs more depth and flexibility, like going off in whatever direction you want or visit the moon or just venturing into space abit further than it is.

Something.

reviews.review_title: Great game but can’t fully complete
reviews.review_description: The game was working fine until i got to the Orbital transfer section. The computer bugged out and I couldn’t progress further because of it also couldn’t reach the marking on the computer. looked at the other reviews and it said to start from chapter 2 and go on. I did but it’s still bugged.

I played the original eons ago. I like the idea of navigating in space using Apollo era technology and physics like KSP. 

This is not that. This is more like a LEO escape room. But I love the setting and atmosphere. Totally my cup of tea.
I can’t grab anything the controls are not intuitive at all and I can’t see anything when the sun isn’t shining

developer_response.body:
Hi TheJRmight94. We're really sorry you had a bad experience with the game.
First, remember you can change the controls scheme in the menu: some people find that the alternative one works better for them.
Second,  regarding the light, we hoped to have calibrated the "dark side" of the orbit in a proper way. Have you tried to augment the brightness of your quest? 
If can always ping us on discord, where we can try and see if the game performs as intended on your headset.

Hope these will solve at least some of your concerns.

reviews.review_description:
Ok so the positive first...voice acting and visual are nice..music is great

That's all... story? Short... barely 30-60 minutes...dlc? Single missions....not dlc.... It's feels unfinished...so don't bother with game... won't recommend
And the mir station bug...that fail the step .... automatically....and then i need to wait 3 minutes again and again...so don't buy

I love it. The genre and subject matter are what I always longed for in a VR game. 
I would have given it five stars if it weren’t so glitchy and had more (and continuous) updates so we could just keep on playing new chapters and storylines. 
I still love it and will play it again and again.

There's a point where you go to Mir. The tablet tells you to press transfer. There is no transfer button, there is no transfer option on the computer screen. Gone through all the menus. Apparently you are supposed to get a summary, checklist, transfer. Summary is not an option for me.... So unfortunately I am soft locked and the game is effectively over. Watched YouTube videos.... I can't do what they do as it simply doesn't appear for me. It was fun while it lasted though.

Update - tried everything, removing floppy and putting it back in let's me finally get to transfer. Can't pass the level though, get the apogee to green, it says transfer failed, need to recalculate. This take long enough that I'll probably give up and wait for a big fix.  A calculate screen appears after I fail that is inaccessible beforehand.

I love this game, and it’s my favourite. But I have a few issues.
1. When docked to a space station, you can’t go inside which kinda defeats the purpose in docking all together, especially in sandbox mode, it’d be really cool to see this added.
2. A multiplayer mode that puts you in a different and slightly larger ship so it can hold 2 Astronauts, this would be game changing to see implemented into this game.
3. Having the ability to use the docking section as an airlock to be able to perform spacewalks, this could be added for a feature that requires you to do repairs of the ship’s exterior.
The game is amazing overall but these three suggestions could make the game even better.
Thanks.

The game has great potential, but I faced significant issues with the grabbing mechanics. I died multiple times simply because I couldn't grab the bars and move as intended. A game that relies on grabbing objects but fails to execute this mechanic is worthless. 

After the developers responded, I decided to give it another try. I'm not sure if there was an update, but the grabbing mechanics were working better. I found that I had to press the trigger as well. Then, I went into the settings and changed to Interkosmos control instead of Quest, and after that, I didn’t have a single problem with grabbing. 

Thanks to the developers for their response! I was extremely frustrated before because I loved the concept of the game and its potential. Eventually, I was able to finish the training with docking, and now I’m in the middle of a mission. It has been really fun, and I am very pleased with the experience.

developer_response.body:
Hi, we're very sorry you had issues with the grabbing.
Have you tried the other control scheme available in the settings?

reviews.review_description:
If you like flipping switches and pushing buttons... this is the game for you.

The feel and tempo of the game are perfect and you are guided through the complexities of space flight.  But don't think that this makes the game easy.  Staying on top of equipment malfunctions and operational duties can be a challenge, especially when the radio is playing out a passive-aggressive cold war dialogue.  

Just brilliant!

reviews.review_title: A great experience held back by bugs
reviews.review_description:
The game looks great. Controlling the spaceship and fixing malfunctions is fun. I like pushing buttons, pulling levers, flipping switches, all nice and tactile. It's not a full simulation, but it scratches the itch. It's a fun game.

But I encountered a few bugs that made it not fun. Grabbing is hit-or-miss; sometimes works perfectly, sometimes takes 10 tries to grab something like the data pad. In the first mission, there was a leak in a pipe but it wouldn't show up. I reloaded the checkpoint several times, then it did show up but a patch wouldn't apply to it. I had to restart the mission entirely, and then I was able to get past that part. Then a few minutes later, when you start to navigate to the space station, my helmet visor wouldn't come down so I had to navigate without HUD assistance which is very very difficult. Even if I got past that, I can only assume there will be more bugs. I decided to get a refund.
This game is to hard just to dock your space ship to the iss and this game doesn’t allow refunds so go save your money and buy a different game
Some of the levels are just flat out impossible and it’s just so boring that it just sits in the back of your library rotting after you give up on your 56th attempt to find the new satellite

I almost gave this a 4 star cause the storyline I don’t care for much but if you’re a space nerd like me and have always wanted to simulate flying a spacecraft, this is it!! So so fun. I will never stop talking about this game to space flight fans

At first you will get confused on the tutorial! But on your second try doing the tutorial it will become super easy to do! And make this game fun to play this happened to me today lol. Merry Christmas!

this is the best space game I ever played. it’s so realistic and the physics are so accurate. the story mode is challenging but not so hard that you can’t play it, and the graphics are really good. i definitely recommend this, 100% worth the money

reviews.review_title: Why is grabbing anything so frustrating
reviews.review_description: It takes 10 to 20 times to grab items sometimes.  Can't slide back and forth, can't grab items. Very frustrating.

developer_response.body:
Hi wojjed,
We're very sorry you had a bad experience with our game. 
In Options/Controls you can change the controls scheme, which also allows for a different grabbing control. We hope it will suit you better.

reviews.review_description: Just started playing the game.  So far, so good.  Must admit it can be challenging at times, but that’s half the fun.  Interior visuals are good and crisp enough.  If the quest 3 hardware has sufficient power, it would be good to see the developers update the exterior visuals.  The earth visuals are pretty low res and it would be great if the developers could sharpen them up a bit.  Lighting effects from cities etc would look great if that can be done.  I noticed The background stars also look stretched when looking through one of the windows.  Overall a good game so far, but would encourage the developers to update the graphics where it possible to do so.  Cheers
its alright i like the interactions i wish they made a mode where you can do a liftoff in sandbox and spawn docked to like the iss that would be awesome

I really like this experience. Ship has many things to tinker with, and is made really well. Mission is funny, immersive, but overall game is really too short. You will need max few hours to complete tutorial and mission. Replayability is for sure a thing here. This game begs for something like user made missions. After first completion I was like "THATS ALL???".

developer_response.body: Hi Doman, we're stoked that you liked the experience! And excited to tell you that you have more to look forward to. We're working hard on a Sandbox Mode that will open up the game, it'll be patched in as soon as it's ready. You'll be able to calculate orbital transfers, which will add a new element to the gameplay, and make it possible to play with no guidance and... well, we don't want to spoil the surprise! We think you'll like it!

reviews.review_description: I like this game I over all would play it every day but I can’t get rid of the drift in sandbox mode so if I could get help with that I would be good.

developer_response.body:
Hello and thank you for kind words!

Reducing the drift is quite tricky indeed, thats why Training Missions are always accessible :)

If you have any more issues and questions, You can always reach us on our discord server - https://discord.gg/9MmN4BADY5

reviews.review_description: At first I skipped the tutorial and didn’t know how to fly but I got the hang of it but could you add a snap option to get in the back

developer_response.body:
Hello and we are glad you have fun!
In the Options > Accessibility you can turn on the snap movement by joysticks, I hope this will help.

reviews.review_title: Cool but orbital transfers are bugged
reviews.review_description: I was playing the game and All was great, all the mechanics but then nooooo, the orbital transfer keeps saying “we need to recalculate are maneuvers” but it was aligned!! Fix this please

Very technical game with lots to consider and keep up with. 
Looking forward to more missions. Can’t quite figure the end of the last one out. Eventually tho. 
Thx for all the hard work and dedication.

My apologies for my overly passionate initial rating and review. And on the plus side, I never gave a hoot enough to take the time to review any other game. I enjoy this game so intensely that in combination with the frustration of that particular mission, the repetitiveness of the instruction did not warrant my response. ￼
I couldn’t even read your responses because I was so utterly embarrassed from my behavior. I appreciate your immediate attention to this particular matter even though I don’t or didn’t deserve it personally. I thought I was passed that sort of “passion” 30 years ago￼￼ lol. 
I hope you can accept my humble apology￼
-N

Stop thinking about it, just buy it. Such a cool game & so much detail. 

Definitely in my top 5 favourites

As someone who’s interested in space and has played ksp before its a pretty awesome game but it could use some more playtime and today I encountered a glitch where my hands in the game get stuck and i am not able to move them around. I first thought it was my oculus not being able to track but my room light was on but after a while it fixed it self other than that glitch and the short play time i really like this game i would recommend it.

Loved this game and can't wait to replay it.

Cockpit and controls are super immersive, story and radio conversations are funny and entertaining.

I've played other spaceflight simulators in the past so I was already familiar with a lot of the navigation concepts.

It's pretty short but a great VR experience.

I just took off the headset, but I'm still in orbit. I loved this experience. Learning to control the ship was just the most delicious mental challenge. I will be playing through this again later because it was just such a blast.
It is a Great game more I don’t have to dat.

Once you get used to this game it's fantastic but at the start it's quite hard so if your starting of on it I would do sandbox mode because it helps you learn how to fly it and missions teach you how to keep your oxygen up the engine stable ect.    But over all this game is fantastic hope you enjoy and don't die
The best game I've played on Oculus, there are some bugs here, but it doesn't interrupt the gameplay. Amazing feeling when you are stoned.  I am looking forward to the update haha.

reviews.review_title: Most engaging quest game but sorta buggy
reviews.review_description: I sometimes find myself having restart mission because the tablet is telling me to do something I already did then going back doing the same thing and it works. Overall I havnt had this much fun in vr in a long time like wow you guys did great. I'm sure the bugs will be resolved Over time I'd totally recommend this if your bored of quest games that feel like cash grabs. This is worth every penny

developer_response.body:
Most engaging Quest game, wow what a compliment! :) Thanks Grady  

we're continuing to work on the game, fixing bugs included (as well as adding a Sandbox Mode)

reviews.review_description: This is not a Review of the game. I Just wanna say thank you for this amazing game and the sandbox mode. I play flight simulator for over ten years or more. This is the most realístic experience i have in my life. Continue with the great work.

developer_response.body:
We humbly accept your praise!
If you are hyped about the Interkosmos 2000 and its future, be sure to join our discord server - https://discord.gg/9MmN4BADY5

reviews.review_description: This could be a brilliant and engaging game. I love the idea of a realistic simulator of a spaceship cockpit. The views are great and it is very immersive. But the game is ruined somewhat by the wooly controls. It is hit and miss whether you grab something or not. I know this is far more positive on other games. Also the pointless malfunctions which require you to endlessly hit panels with a wrench. Whoever thought this a good idea needs a serious word. All it does is frustrates any progress made.
Love the game !

reviews.review_title: Can not start the first step
reviews.review_description: The game will crash after trying to play the first step and I'm hoping this issue is fixed asap. Looks like a fun game

developer_response.body:
Hi ThursdayVallee,
We have pushed an update (1.8) that should fix these problems now. 
Hope you will be able to confirm your feel about the game! 
Godspeed in space!

reviews.review_description:
I thoroughly enjoyed the roughly 3 hours I got to play this game. Unfortunately it was only three hours between training and the mission￼… for $20 I would’ve hoped for a bit more but I guess I can’t be overly disappointed. I’m still rating it at five stars and hopefully they don’t do the same thing others have done and charge $20 per mission but I guess we will see what happens.
Overall good job Ovid Works.

i played on immersive mode and i loved it since i am obsessed with space, you have to do all the small things in the ship like, change co2 filters and fuses which is kinda cool.

so if you want to feel like a cosmonaut buy it with my code for 25% off

https://www.ocuIus.com/appreferrals/Leo_Kliesen_0/4129713367142723/?utm_source=2
